title: Infrastructure hardening round: encoding, UI-contract, visual, and structure mechanical gates
goal: Land four mechanical CI gates (encoding integrity, DESIGN.md §7 UI-contract, Playwright visual regression, REPOSITORY_LAYOUT structure/naming) plus the SearchToolbar rollout and menu-tree filter fix, so that recurring drift classes (mojibake, off-contract UI, layout regressions, misplaced files) are caught by CI instead of by human review.

in: vendor check-encoding gate from pantheon-harness into scripts/harness and wire blocking in quality.yml
add frontend/scripts/check-ui-contract.mjs sweeping DESIGN.md §7.6/7.7/7.9 forbidden patterns with ui-contract-allow escape hatch, wired into prebuild
add Playwright visual-regression project (playwright.visual.config.ts + tests/visual) with committed win32 baselines
add scripts/harness/check-structure-contract.mjs enforcing REPOSITORY_LAYOUT.md §2 placement and naming, wired blocking in quality.yml docs-governance
roll out SearchToolbar to all list pages, generator, and keyword APIs; menu tree text filter keeps ancestor chain
add three-touchpoint maintainer contract to CLAUDE.md
out: pantheon-ops synchronization (tracked separately on the ops branch)
linux visual baselines (must be generated in a linux runner before CI enforcement)
the uncommitted SearchToolbar UI working-tree WIP owned by a separate session

taskDoc: none — maintainer-directed infrastructure hardening executed across the session; decision record in this manifest, evidence summary, and project memory infra_hardening_2026_07_17.md

doNotTouch: the separate session's uncommitted SearchToolbar UI working-tree changes

evidenceRequired: gate self-check results (0 findings at landing)
humanGates: maintainer requested the four mechanical gates to stop UI and layout drift from reaching review
maintainer established the three-touchpoint contract governing autonomous execution between touchpoints
